topic: ChatGPT prompting strategies and real-world use cases

entries.a: Prompts.chat is the world's largest open-source prompt library for AI, providing a curated collection of prompt examples that work with various AI chat models including ChatGPT, Claude, and Llama.

entries.q: How can I contribute to prompts.chat?
entries.a: You can contribute by adding prompts at prompts.chat/prompts/new, where they will sync automatically to the library.

entries.q: What is the Interactive Book of Prompting?
entries.a: The Interactive Book of Prompting is a free, interactive guide with over 25 chapters that cover everything from basic to advanced prompting techniques, including chain-of-thought reasoning and few-shot learning.

entries.q: What age group is the 'Prompting for Kids' program designed for?
entries.a: The 'Prompting for Kids' program is designed for children ages 8-14, using interactive, game-based adventures to teach them how to communicate with AI.

entries.q: What are some of the advanced prompting techniques covered in the Interactive Book?
entries.a: The Interactive Book covers advanced techniques such as chain-of-thought reasoning and few-shot learning, which enhance the effectiveness of AI interactions.

entries.q: How can I self-host my own prompt library using prompts.chat?
entries.a: You can self-host by running 'npx prompts.chat new my-prompt-library' to create a new library, followed by 'cd my-prompt-library' to navigate into it. A manual setup is also available via GitHub.

entries.q: What integrations are available with prompts.chat?
entries.a: Prompts.chat offers integrations such as a CLI tool, a Claude Code Plugin, and the ability to use it as an MCP server in AI tools.
